signals.headline: **Key Confirmation**: Sources report the US and Iran are considering fresh negotiations, potentially as early as this we
signals.raw: **Key Confirmation**: Sources report the US and Iran are considering fresh negotiations, potentially as early as this week in Pakistan, to secure a longer-term ceasefire deal before the current two-week truce ends around April 21; this follows failed talks last weekend and amid proposals for suspending Iran's nuclear activities (e.g., Iran's 5-year offer vs. US 20-year demand).[1][2]

signals.raw: **Market Impact Potential**: Talks boost ceasefire expectations (stocks up, oil down), but blockade escalates tensions, offsetting optimism; no final resolution yet, just "weighing" per insiders—insufficient for full resolution but notable for prediction markets on ceasefires or escalations.[1]

signals.headline: South Korea Annual Inflation Rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea Annual Inflation Rises to 2.2% in March 2026, Up from 2.0% but Below 2.4% Forecasts | YES | HIGH confidence** [1]

This figure, just released by Statistics Korea, marks the first uptick in months after holding steady at 2% in January and February 2026—aligning with the Bank of Korea's 2% target amid Middle East energy volatility, though core pressures may build per KDI outlook.[1][2][4
